decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressive strategies with the ability to repeatedly blink or flicker creatures for added value. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck can protect its creatures or trigger powerful enter-the-battlefield effects multiple times. The deck also includes a mix of control elements with cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with efficient creatures and disruptive spells, while generating incremental advantage through blinking and flickering synergies.

The deck focuses on combining Goryo's Vengeance with powerful creatures like Griselbrand to quickly reanimate them from the graveyard and win the game. It uses cards like Faithful Mending, Ephemerate, and Solitude to protect its key pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to control the game and protect its combo. Atraxa, Grand Unifier helps to accelerate the deck and provide additional value. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win before the opponent can react.

The deck focuses on using Eldrazi creatures and ramp spells to control the game and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. It utilizes cards like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths to quickly close out games, while also utilizing control elements like Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and World Breaker for additional late-game power and resilience. By ramping into big threats and disrupting the opponent's strategy, this deck aims to dominate the battlefield and secure victory.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bring them back onto the battlefield using reanimation spells like Persist and Unearth. By combining cards like Faithless Looting, Thought Scour, and Thoughtseize to quickly fill up the graveyard while disrupting the opponent's gameplan, this deck aims to bring out big threats like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones in a single turn, overwhelming the opponent and securing victory through graveyard manipulation and powerful reanimation abilities.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay by utilizing creatures like Ragaven, Nimble Pilferer, Ocelot Pride, and Guide of Souls to deal damage quickly. Support cards like Galvanic Discharge and Boromir, Warden of the Tower provide removal and control options. The deck also includes some control elements with Static Prison and Ranger-Captain of Eos to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced and powerful offense while still being able to control the game when needed.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game without relying on traditional lands. This deck focuses on using various counter spells and disruption cards to control the game and protect the combo pieces. The key cards in this deck include Goblin Charbelcher, Hydroelectric Specimen, and Tameshi, Reality Architect, which work together to create a powerful win condition. By using a combination of card draw, tutoring, and control elements, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents and secure a victory through its unique combo strategy.

This aggro deck focuses on using efficient creatures and removal spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent and deal damage. The key concept revolves around establishing a strong early board presence with cheap creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while using cards like Fatal Push and Goblin Bombardment to control the opponent's board and remove threats. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide additional utility and support for the aggressive game plan. The deck aims to apply constant pressure and quickly close out games before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost spells and efficient creatures, such as Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler. By utilizing cards like Lava Dart and Lightning Bolt to clear the way and pump spells like Mutagenic Growth to buff your creatures, the deck aims to take control of the game early on and finish off the opponent before they have a chance to respond. The deck also includes card draw and selection to maintain momentum and ensure a consistent stream of threats.

The deck focuses on a combination of aggressive creatures and cards that allow for repeated flickering or blinking to trigger powerful effects. By continually flickering creatures like Guide of Souls, Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, and Quantum Riddler with cards like Ephemerate and Solitude, the deck can generate significant value and overwhelm opponents. This strategy is supplemented by cards like Springleaf Drum and March of Otherworldly Light to maintain tempo and pressure on the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue opponents through efficient creature interactions and consistent aggression.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells. Utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and apply pressure to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Solitude and Goblin Bombardment offers removal options against opposing threats, while supporting cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Elegant Parlor provide additional value and versatility. Additionally, cards like Blood Moon and The Legend of Roku can disrupt opponents' game plans and give this aggro deck an edge in the Modern format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly establish a strong board presence and apply pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to deal with threats and maintain control of the battlefield. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos allows for additional versatility and card advantage, while cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Thraben Charm provide flexibility in different situations. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and removal spells to secure a quick victory.

The main idea of this deck is to assemble a powerful combo involving cards like Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and Blade of the Bloodchief to generate massive amounts of value and overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Ancient Stirrings and Urza's Saga to tutor for key pieces, along with disruption like Malevolent Rumble and Pithing Needle to control the game, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and go for the win using its powerful synergy and combo potential.

The main strategy of this deck revolves around combining the abilities of Basking Broodscale, Blade of the Bloodchief, and Glaring Fleshraker to create a powerful increasing threat that can overwhelm opponents. The deck also uses cards like Ancient Stirrings, Kozilek's Command, and Malevolent Rumble to control the board and disrupt opponent's strategies. With the support of cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Ugin's Labyrinth, the deck aims to search for essential combo pieces and protect its creatures while building towards summoning Emrakul, the Promised End for a game-winning finish.

The deck focuses on using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to quickly bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand to the battlefield and generate a significant advantage. It also includes cards that disrupt the opponent's game plan, such as Force of Negation and Thoughtseize. The deck aims to assemble the combo pieces as quickly as possible and overwhelm the opponent with early threats and powerful reanimation spells. By leveraging the graveyard and instant speed interactions, the deck can surprise opponents with explosive plays and close out games swiftly.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly fill your graveyard with powerful creatures and then bring them back onto the battlefield using various reanimation spells such as Unearth and Persist. By using cards like Faithless Looting and Thought Scour to dump creatures into the graveyard, you can cheat powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones into play early on and overwhelm your opponent. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce to help protect your combo and disrupt your opponent's game plan.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly generate mana and card advantage through spells like Manamorphose, Pyretic Ritual, and Ruby Medallion, while also utilizing Ral, Monsoon Mage's abilities to filter through the deck and deal damage. The deck aims to cast multiple spells in a turn to build towards a combo finish with Grapeshot for lethal damage. By utilizing cards like Glimpse the Impossible and Wish, the deck can dig for key pieces and set up explosive turns to overwhelm the opponent.
